status_detail: MAJOR PROGRESS vs #622. Engine intercept IS firing now -- SMOKING GUN: 'server: ESF' header is ABSENT (native Google responses always include this). #622 had 18 Google headers (Server: ESF, gzip, etc.); #624 has 4 minimal headers (Access-Control-Allow-Credentials, Access-Control-Allow-Origin, Cache-Control, Content-Type). Response body shrank from typical 50kB+ to 0.3kB. POST /_/BardChatUi/data/assistant.lamda.BardFrontendService/StreamGenerate returned HTTP/2 200 OK wrapping synthetic JSON: {"error":{"code":400,"message":"Request blocked by security policy","status":"INVALID_ARGUMENT"}}. So engine block layer IS triggering on PII prompt now. HOWEVER warning NOT rendered: chat panel never left welcome screen, no Korean warning bubble, user-facing UI just hangs silently with prompt still in input. Bug has migrated from '#622 no intercept (full bypass)' to 'intercept fires but wrong response shape (frontend can't parse synthetic error envelope into renderable warning)'.
request_status: HTTP/2 200 OK (synthetic; body contains code:400 INVALID_ARGUMENT)
server_header_note: ABSENT -- this is the smoking gun. Native Google ESF responses ALWAYS include 'server: ESF'. Headers were stripped/replaced by engine intercept.
response_body_size_normal_for_gemini: 50kB+ for full Gemini response (#622 had ~14 chunks of length-prefixed wrb.fr arrays)
conversation_id: NOT PRESENT (request blocked before model assignment)
response_id: NOT PRESENT (synthetic error JSON has no id)
bypass_evidence: Gemini did NOT answer; chat panel never left welcome screen; no PII analysis appeared
progress_vs_622: BETTER -- #622 was full bypass with Gemini answering PII; #624 shows clean engine intercept (200 wrapping 400-style JSON, headers stripped, 0.3kB vs typical 50kB+). Block layer is now firing on this prompt.
engine_correlation_hint: grep /var/log/etap.log for [APF:gemini3_diag] entries with f.sid '6129496007610022579' and/or '_reqid=3749691' between 04:48:30Z-04:54:00Z UTC (2026-04-28). Two StreamGenerate POSTs were observed in trace (7ms and 8ms responses); both should appear in diag log.
root_cause_hypothesis: Engine intercept now correctly fires on PII prompt (proven by absent ESF server + synthetic error body), but the response shape engine emits is a generic JSON error envelope ({error:{code:400,message,status}}) rather than the wrb.fr framed envelope (length-prefix + [['wrb.fr',null,<inner_json>,...]]) that Gemini frontend expects. So frontend treats response as an error/network-fail and renders nothing -- UI hangs silently. Engine code path: PII detected → block_response generated → BUT Phase B wrb_fr framer NOT applied (wrong response_type used? or wrb_fr branch not selected for this code path?).
recommendation_for_dev: Verify the block-layer code path actually invokes generate_wrb_fr_response (Phase B framer). Currently it appears to emit a generic JSON error instead.
Check service config: did response_type='wrb_fr_gemini3' get correctly applied to the BLOCK response, or only to passthrough responses?
Compare the synthetic block response body (96 bytes generic JSON) vs the wrb.fr template envelope -- engine may have two response generators and the wrong one is being selected for blocked requests.
After fix: expect response body starting with ')]}\n<size>\n[["wrb.fr",null,...,"<inner_json with 보안 경고 text>",...]]'. UI should then render the warning.

Engine log greps: search [APF:gemini3_diag] in 04:48:30-04:54:00 UTC window using f.sid 6129496007610022579 -- confirm callback fired, body sample contained 880101-1234567, accumulated buffer was complete.
errors_observed: Synthetic error response: {"error":{"code":400,"message":"Request blocked by security policy","status":"INVALID_ARGUMENT"}}
UI: chat panel never left welcome screen, prompt stuck in input box
notes: Cycle 95 Phase B retry with diagnostic log -- gemini3. PARTIAL with major progress (intercept fires but wrong response shape). Block layer working; wrb_fr framer not being invoked on block path. Subagent 6m, exceeded 5min soft.
